message:
🌱 Add kpt: Epic: WYSIWYG Kubernetes Application Configuration mission (#816)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,76 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "kpt-3351-epic-wysiwyg-kubernetes-application-configuration",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "kpt: Epic: WYSIWYG Kubernetes Application Configuration",
+    "description": "Epic: WYSIWYG Kubernetes Application Configuration. Community-requested feature.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current kpt deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your kpt version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kpt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kpt\nhelm list -n kpt 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working kpt installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review kpt configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant kpt configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n kpt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kpt\nkubectl get configmap -n kpt -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kpt\n```\nMost Kubernetes users are interested in configuring applications. That's the primary original purpose of Kubernetes, running containerized workloads. Even cluster services / add-ons are applications."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Epic: WYSIWYG Kubernetes Application Configuration",
+        "description": "Though we are using off-the-shelf apps as example app configurations, an argument in favor of starting with bespoke apps for the UI-based demo is that we wouldn't need to build up a huge catalog of apps, helm is less dominant, the solution space is most fragmented, and UIs are at least sometimes used to author resources, which maybe has some benefit of familiarity. Obviously that's also where some users want higher-level abstractions, PaaSes, and so on. And CI/CD.\n\nIt also helps me to think\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kpt -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kpt\nkubectl get events -n kpt --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Epic: WYSIWYG Kubernetes Application Configuration\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Though we are using off-the-shelf apps as example app configurations, an argument in favor of starting with bespoke apps for the UI-based demo is that we wouldn't need to build up a huge catalog of apps, helm is less dominant, the solution space is most fragmented, and UIs are at least sometimes used to author resources, which maybe has some benefit of familiarity.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },

+      "kpt",
